mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels above 315 trigger anticipatory monetary tightening expectations, causing immediate risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are non-negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 315, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days consistently across backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indices reflect stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction, reducing aggregate consumption forecasts and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by > 50k in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of months following a PAYEMS decline greater than 50,000, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined positive momentum of GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns, with a cumulative drawdown exceeding 5% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Tight labor markets below 3.8% unemployment combined with rising industrial production indicate supply-side bottlenecks that force corporate pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns turn negative when UNRATE falls below 3.8% alongside rising INDPRO, or the interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.
